name: Moroccan Style Quinoa
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-quart pot, bring chickpeas, kombu and soaking water to a boil. Cover, reduce heat to medium and boil for 2 hours. Remove the liquid from the pot and set aside in a 2-quart pot.
2. Place onions, carrots, and turnips on top of the chickpeas and add enough water or stock to just cover vegetables. Add salt, garlic, bay leaf, cumin, pepper, and olive oil. Cover and bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat to a light boil and cook for 40 minutes. Add Brussels sprouts and cook an additional 10 minutes. Adjust seasoning as needed.
4. While the vegetables and chickpeas continue to cook, add water and salt to the stock in the 2-quart pot and bring to a boil.
5. Heat oil in a small pan, then toast quinoa for 10 minutes. Add the toasted quinoa to the boiling stock, cover, and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes.
6. Remove from heat and let sit for 5 to 10 minutes. Gently mix from top to bottom with a damp wooden spoon. Cover pot and let rest for another 5 to 10 minutes.
7. Serve by placing a large mound of quinoa on each individual plate. Flatten the mound in the center and fill with vegetables and chickpeas. Pour about half a cup of hot stock over all and serve hot.
